personal.name: KMohZaid
personal.tagline: A passionate developer specializing in backend systems, open source contributions, and building tools that make developers' lives easier.

site.title: KMohZaid | Backend Developer & Open Source Contributor
site.description: Backend developer specializing in distributed systems, microservices, and open source contributions.

mastering.name: Python
mastering.description: I started learning Python in 2021 specifically to create a Userbot on Telegram, adding custom features as I learned. Now it's my go-to language for scripting, automation, and backend APIs.

mastering.name: JavaScript
mastering.description: I learned JavaScript alongside HTML/CSS specifically for building React applications, as I never enjoyed building websites without it. Now it's a constant companion for full-stack development.

mastering.name: Node.js
mastering.description: My favorite backend environment. I'm good at it, and it provides me with the JavaScript execution I need. I've built many applications with it, and my experience so far has been solid.

mastering.name: SQL Databases
mastering.description: PostgreSQL is my life-saver for speed and performance. I learned Oracle DB concepts during my diploma, though I don't use it much now. I've used various clients like MySQL Workbench, DBeaver, and PHPMyAdmin to manage databases efficiently.

mastering.name: MongoDB
mastering.description: Very useful back when I was building userbots and my SQL knowledge wasn't solid. I still use it when I need a free tier for hosting a database online quickly.

mastering.name: Git
mastering.description: Started as a skill I found on YouTube, and now I love it. Creating branches, organizing commits, rolling back changes, and seeing exactly what changed when is essential to my workflow.

mastering.name: Linux
mastering.description: I always wanted to switch but was too comfortable with Windows. When I planned to buy a new laptop, I decided to switch, but my motivation peaked early—I backed up my data to an external drive, wiped Windows, and installed Linux daily. Now, I never feel like going back.

mastering.name: Bash
mastering.description: The best terminal shell. I use it for writing deploy scripts, CI pipelines, and system tooling.

mastering.name: Docker
mastering.description: Helps me deploy my applications easily. I'm good at containerizing everything for consistent environments.

mastering.name: RESTful APIs
mastering.description: Building and consuming APIs is second nature. I'm comfortable with standard methods, handling responses, and integrating services.

learned_for_now.name: React
learned_for_now.description: I know the basics and can build apps, but I haven't mastered it yet.

learned_for_now.name: TypeScript
learned_for_now.description: Provides the types I want to prevent future errors. It's good for developer experience and safer code.

learned_for_now.name: Next.js
learned_for_now.description: I can use it, but I'm not fully proficient in all its advanced features.

learned_for_now.name: Java
learned_for_now.description: I learned Java, so I know it. Used mainly for Android development.

learned_for_now.name: Android Dev
learned_for_now.description: I know Android Studio and have built applications with it.

learned_for_now.name: Telegram Bots
learned_for_now.description: I build Telegram bots, a skill I've honed since starting my coding journey with userbots.

working_in_progress.name: Data Science & ML
working_in_progress.description: Currently expanding my knowledge in AI, ML, and Data Science fields.

working_in_progress.name: CyberSecurity
working_in_progress.description: Currently learning offensive security concepts and participating in CTF competitions.

working_in_progress.name: Go
working_in_progress.description: Trying to learn Go because of its high performance in the backend. It's extremely popular among developers right now, and I've learned the very basics.

working_in_progress.name: DevOps
working_in_progress.description: Since I already know Docker, I consider myself learning DevOps. I want to expand into other tools and methodologies.

working_in_progress.name: Nginx
working_in_progress.description: Learning it to set up reverse proxies, map custom ports to my Docker containers, and hide my actual server IP behind a proxy or merge servers under one domain.

on_my_radar.name: Rust
on_my_radar.description: Planning to learn it because it's cool, fast, and speeds up tooling.

on_my_radar.name: AWS
on_my_radar.description: Cloud infrastructure is popular and essential. I plan to learn the core services hands-on.

on_my_radar.name: GraphQL
on_my_radar.description: Planning to learn it because it's cool I heard.

on_my_radar.name: Redis
on_my_radar.description: Planning to learn it properly to speed up my applications with caching.
